subject: Human Resources
title: The Americans with Disabilities Act
description: A quick win: understand The Americans with Disabilities Act.

hook.text: A qualified accountant who uses a wheelchair is rejected because the office 'isn't accessible.' The ADA says: fix the office.

buildup.text: The ADA prohibits discrimination against qualified individuals with disabilities in all employment practices.

discovery.text: It requires 'reasonable accommodations'—modifications that allow disabled employees to perform essential job functions.

twist.text: Accommodations are often inexpensive—ergonomic equipment, flexible schedules, screen readers. Most cost under $500.

climax.text: The ADA's interactive process requires employers and employees to collaborate on solutions—not just deny requests.

punchline.text: The ADA doesn't lower the bar. It removes the barriers.

quiz.question: What does the ADA require employers to provide?
options: Reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities
